message:
feat(agents): add TTY guard to prevent control sequence leakage in robot mode

Problem:
When running bv with --robot-* flags in certain PTY/TTY capture environments
(notably agent runners like Claude Code), Bubble Tea's initialization triggers
Lipgloss/Termenv background detection. This emits OSC/DSR control sequences
to stdout, which are harmless in a real terminal but break JSON parsers
consuming robot-mode output.

Solution:
Add a new tty_guard.go package with an init() function that runs before
Bubble Tea acquires the terminal. When robot-mode invocation is detected
(via --robot-* flags, BV_ROBOT=1, or BV_TEST_MODE env vars), the guard
sets CI=1 early. Termenv uses the CI environment variable to disable
TTY probing, preventing those sequences from being written.

Detection triggers:
- Any argument starting with --robot-
- --version or --help flags (non-interactive)
- BV_ROBOT=1 environment variable
- BV_TEST_MODE being set (any value)

The guard is idempotent - if CI is already set, it exits early.

Includes comprehensive test coverage for shouldSuppressTTYQueries() with
table-driven tests covering all detection scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
